todos.content: Create 6 v1.1 week milestones on GitHub
File new issues for design-captured items scheduled into the cycle (#587–#596)
Move scheduled existing issues onto week milestones
Add cycle issues to DataQ Roadmap project + v1.1 week field + epic #597
Update docs/progress.md cycle plan + CLAUDE.md §13 headline on a branch
PR #598 open, review clean, CI green — merge blocked by permissions, awaiting user
todos.activeForm: Opening and merging the planning PR

result.output: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

content.text:
Yes — I agree with prioritizing PATs, with one carve-out. Here's my thinking, plus a status note you need first.

**Status:** PR [#598](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/598) (the cycle-plan docs) is fully ready — review clean, all 14 CI checks green — but the permission system blocked me from squash-merging my own PR to protected `main`, so it's waiting for you to hit merge (or tell me explicitly to). All the GitHub state (milestones `v1.1 Week 1..6`, issues #587–#596, epic #597, the `DataQ Roadmap` project wiring) is already live.

**On PATs (#461):** you're right that it's the biggest unlocker in the backlog, and the subscription clock actually strengthens the case:

- It's the only thing that breaks the Azure-AD-only auth dependency **before** Azure winds down (W3). Once landed, every later live check — the W3 final E2E, `e2e_smoke.py`, the MCP NL spot-check #584 — runs on a DataQ bearer instead of the Azure-CLI token workaround (#565).
- It has to land **while Azure AD still works as the reference validator** — you mint a PAT from a logged-in session, so the Entra window is its natural deadline. Doing it early in the window beats doing it at the edge.
- It's the hard dependency for checks-as-code, headless MCP clients, and the whole ADR 0013 BYOL story — and the v1 retro itself said "schedule PATs early if programmatic/live use grows."
- Your "1 admin + 1 member" framing maps exactly onto the ADR 0026 shape: PATs are **user-scoped and inherit the owner's grants**, so you'd mint one as the workspace-admin user and one as a member (e.g. Olivia) — which conveniently re-creates the two-tier authz matrix from the go-live workout for all future headless testing. One caution: the admin PAT inherits workspace-admin powers, so give it a short expiry.

**The one thing that should still go first:** the Snowflake-live trio (#194/#195 live-verify + #587 baseline). That deadline is measured in *days*, not weeks — once the subscription lapses those become permanently un-verifiable, while PATs stay doable for the full 3-week Azure window. It's also only ~1–2 days of work before PATs get the rest of the week.

**Concrete reshuffle I'd make:** move #461 from W2 → **W1** (right behind the Snowflake trio, with #588's retirement of the leg able to run after), and push #532 (dry-run UC/flat-file — fully cloud-independent, no deadline) from W1 → W2 to balance. W2 then becomes OTel + secrets lifecycle + #584 running *on* the new PATs.

Since #598 is still unmerged, I can fold this into the same PR (progress.md + milestone + epic + project field updates) in one pass. Want me to?
On it. Reshuffling #461 → W1 and #532 → W2 across the milestones, project, epic, and the PR branch.

input.command:
gh api -X PATCH repos/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/milestones/10 -f title="v1.1 Week 1 — Snowflake close-out + PATs" -f description="Snowflake subscription lapses within days — day-1 priority: live-verify key-pair work (#194/#195), capture a Snowflake scale/volume baseline (#587, G-b datum), then retire the harness Snowflake leg cleanly (#588). Right behind the Snowflake trio: PATs phase 1 (#461, ADR 0026) — pulled forward from W2 because it breaks the Azure-AD-only auth dependency early in the Azure window and every later live check (W2 #584, W3 final E2E) runs on the minted admin+member PATs. Plus MCP profile_column run-target default (#583)." -q '.title'
gh api -X PATCH repos/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/milestones/11 -f title="v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth" -f description="Azure-verified-while-it-exists portability: opencensus→OTel log migration (#524) + generic OTLP exporter endpoint (#589) verified against App Insights; SecretStore.delete on the live Key Vault (#372); dry-run preview beyond Snowflake — UC + flat-file (#532, moved from W1: cloud-independent, no deadline); MCP NL tool-selection spot-check vs live /mcp (#584) — running on the W1 PATs." -q '.title'
gh api -X PATCH repos/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/461 -F milestone=10 -q '"#461 -> \(.milestone.title)"'
gh api -X PATCH repos/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/532 -F milestone=11 -q '"#532 -> \(.milestone.title)"'
input.description: Swap milestones for #461 and #532, retitle W1/W2

### v1.1 W1 — Snowflake close-out + dry-run depth (due 2026-07-11) — 0/6

within days; after it does they can no longer be live-verified.

| Status | Task | Theme / gap |

Land the vendor-neutral seams **while App Insights / Key Vault / live `/mcp` still exist to
verify parity against** (ADR 0010/0013/0028 discipline; retro flagged PATs for early scheduling).

### v1.1 W1 — Snowflake close-out + PATs (due 2026-07-11) — 0/6

⚡ **The three Snowflake-live rows are day-1 work, in this order** — the subscription lapses
within days; after it does they can no longer be live-verified. **PATs (#461) start right
behind them** (pulled forward from W2 at planning, 2026-07-04): the biggest unlocker in the
backlog, and it must land while Azure AD is still the reference validator.

| Status | Task | Theme / gap |
|---|---|---|
| ⬜ | [#194](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/194) Snowflake key-pair: encrypted (passphrase-protected) private keys — **live-verify** | Theme 8 |
| ⬜ | [#195](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/195) Snowflake key-pair: migrate off deprecated GX `connect_args` path — **live-verify** | Theme 8 |
| ⬜ | [#587](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/587) Snowflake scale/volume baseline (harness §6 volume) — the pushdown-path reference datum for W6's G-b work | Theme 7 / G-b |

content.text:
The swap is fully applied everywhere; CI is re-running on the new commit in the background. What changed:

- **GitHub milestones**: W1 retitled **"v1.1 Week 1 — Snowflake close-out + PATs"**, W2 → **"…OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth"**; #461 now on W1, #532 on W2, descriptions updated with the rationale.
- **Project + epic**: `v1.1 week` field swapped for both items; epic [#597](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/597) checklists moved with notes.
- **#461** got a comment recording the pull-forward rationale and the acceptance detail you asked for: mint **1 workspace-admin + 1 member PAT** after phase 1 lands (two-tier authz matrix for headless testing; short expiry on the admin one since it inherits ADR-0027 workspace-admin powers).
- **Docs**: progress.md W1/W2 tables + CLAUDE.md §13 updated and pushed to PR #598 as a `fixup:` commit; PR body refreshed.

W1's order of operations is now: the three Snowflake-live items day 1 (that clock is in days), #588 retirement at lapse, then PATs get the rest of the week. I'll report when CI is green — the merge itself is yours (I'm permission-blocked from merging my own PR to protected `main`).
